methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $28M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)

data.narrative:
Orbit Fab, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Daniel Faber and Jeremy Schiel, has raised $28.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network) valued between $400M and $2.0B, plus 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $57M on the $28.5M capital raised proxy. Peer-median anchors to the 4-company comparable set, which carries a median valuation of $850M (range: $400M–$2.0B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable owing to the absence of disclosed revenue for the private target. Public Space & Aerospace medians (42.9x P/S across 6 tickers) would require known revenue to operationalize.

The two applicable methods produce a wide range. The triangulated midpoint sits at $454M, with a low bound of $48M (driven by stage-progression) and a high bound of $977M (peer-median ceiling). This disparity reflects material uncertainty: whether Orbit Fab should anchor to fundraising history or to peer valuations across heterogeneous Series A and Series B stages. Revenue visibility and customer traction remain critical unknowns for narrowing the band.
